header_label: Aula 7 — At the Hotel: Checking In and Asking for Help
card_title: At the Hotel — Checking In and Asking for Help
card_sub: Reservations & requests (would like / need to / have to)
accordion_desc: Checking In and Asking for Help: would like to / need to / have to
welcome:
Welcome back, Pricila!

Onboarding

You landed in Sydney and found your way out of the airport. This week you arrive at your hotel: how to check in, ask for the room you want, and solve problems — with would like to, need to, and have to. Review these emergency phrases before class.

I have a reservation under the name Adamo.
I would like a room with a view, please.
What time do I have to check out?
Is breakfast included?
audit:
<!--
SCENARIO FIT — Aula 7
Can-do: "I can check in at a hotel, ask for the room and services I want, and report a problem using would like to, need to, and have to."
Gramatica-alvo: would like (+ noun / + to + verb) = desejo educado; need to + verb = necessidade; have to + verb = obrigacao/regra.
Vocab-alvo: reception, reservation, key card, check-out, double room, floor, amenities, complaint.
Cenario escolhido: recepcao de hotel (Pricila chega do aeroporto e faz o check-in no Sydney Harbour Hotel; depois reporta um problema no quarto).
Por que elicita o alvo: o balcao da recepcao OBRIGA o aluno a dizer o que quer (I would like a room with a view), o que precisa (I need to change my room) e a entender as regras (You have to check out by 11). >70% dos itens-alvo (8 vocab + 3 estruturas) sao naturalmente elicitados. Habitat natural do focoLinguistico.

CONTINUIDADE — Aula 7
Itens novos desta aula: reception, reservation, key card, check-out, double room, floor, amenities, complaint; would like to / need to / have to.
Itens revisados (de aulas anteriores): pedidos educados da Aula 6 (Could I...? / Could you...?), vocab de aeroporto (luggage, check-in) e a jornada de viagem (Aulas 3/4).
Callback no warm-up (slide 2): retoma ATIVAMENTE 2 itens da Aula 6 — (1) o aluno produz um pedido educado ("Could I have...?") e (2) usa o vocab "luggage/check-in" para fazer a ponte do aeroporto ate a recepcao do hotel.
NB: nenhum item-alvo novo repete vocabulario ja ensinado nas Aulas 1-6.
-->

challenges.prompt: At reception, say you have a booking.
challenges.answer: "I have a reservation under the name Adamo."

challenges.prompt: Ask politely for a room with a view.
challenges.answer: "I would like a room with a view, please."

challenges.prompt: Ask what time you must leave the hotel.
challenges.answer: "What time do I have to check out?"

challenges.prompt: Your key card does not work. Report it politely.
challenges.answer: "Excuse me, my key card is not working. Could you help me?"

challenges.prompt: Say you need to print something.
challenges.answer: "I need to print a document. Is there a business center?"
